title: Local-community handover + validator training
context: open-map-gaps's durable goal is that local OSM communities own their maps. Remote contribution from Hee-Lee Oss is scaffolding, not the destination. Once at least one region has packs validated and merged, the pipeline and validation workflow must be handed to a local mapper/community with a trained validator who can sign off independently, so the work continues without Hee-Lee Oss. The handover document covers the gates, tagging profile, channel setup, and Organised Editing registration.
objective: Produce and deliver a local-community handover document and validator training materials for at least one region, enabling an independent local validator to operate without Hee-Lee Oss support.
acceptanceCriteria: At least one region's pack/validation workflow is handed to a local OSM community with a trained validator able to sign off independently.

Handover document covers: gate procedures (imagery-gate-002, harm-gate-003), tagging profile (tagging-005), contribution channel setup (TM/MapRoulette/Notes), and OSMF Organised Editing registration steps.
Trained validator is named and has successfully completed at least one independent validation sign-off for that region.
Document is committed and reviewed by the Steward and experienced OSM validator.
output: A committed handover document and validator training guide for at least one region, with the trained validator named and evidence of an independent sign-off recorded.
